title: Received an `Unknown column` error when using recursive CTE
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->
```
CREATE TABLE some_table (
	id INTEGER NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
	data VARCHAR(50),
	parent_id INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (id),
	FOREIGN KEY(parent_id) REFERENCES some_table (id)
)

INSERT INTO some_table (id, data, parent_id) VALUES (1, 'd1', NULL),(2, 'd2', 1),(3, 'd3', 1),(4, 'd4', 3),(5, 'd5', 3)

WITH recursive some_cte(id, data, parent_id)
AS
  (
         SELECT some_table.id        AS id,
                some_table.data      AS data,
                some_table.parent_id AS parent_id
         FROM   some_table
         WHERE  some_table.data IN ('d2',
                                    'd3',
                                    'd4')
         UNION ALL
         SELECT some_table_1.id        AS id,
                some_table_1.data      AS data,
                some_table_1.parent_id AS parent_id
         FROM   some_table             AS some_table_1,
                some_cte               AS c1
         WHERE  some_table_1.id = c1.parent_id
  )
  SELECT   some_cte.data
  FROM     some_cte
  WHERE    some_cte.data != 'd2'
  ORDER BY some_cte.data DESC
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
+------+
| data |
+------+
| d4   |
| d3   |
| d3   |
| d1   |
| d1   |
| d1   |
+------+
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
ERROR 1054 (42S22): Unknown column 'some_cte.data' in 'order clause'
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
 Release Version: v7.2.0
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 9fd5f4a8e4f273a60fbe7d3848f85a1be8f0600b
Git Branch: heads/refs/tags/v7.2.0
UTC Build Time: 2023-06-27 14:56:57
GoVersion: go1.20.5
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: tikv
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE another_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(100), ref_id INTEGER REFERENCES another_table (id));
INSERT INTO another_table (value, ref_id) VALUES ('v1', NULL), ('v2', 1), ('v3', 1), ('v4', 3), ('v5', 3);

WITH RECURSIVE another_cte(id, value, ref_id) AS (SELECT another_table.id, another_table.value, another_table.ref_id FROM another_table WHERE another_table.value IN ('v2', 'v3', 'v4') UNION ALL SELECT at.id, at.value, at.ref_id FROM another_table AS at JOIN another_cte AS ac ON at.id = ac.ref_id) SELECT another_cte.value FROM another_cte WHERE another_cte.value != 'v2' ORDER BY another_cte.value DESC;
CREATE TABLE yet_another_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, description TEXT, parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES yet_another_table (id));
INSERT INTO yet_another_table (description, parent_id) VALUES ('desc1', NULL), ('desc2', 1), ('desc3', 1), ('desc4', 3), ('desc5', 3);
key_bug_features: Ordering by column in recursive CTE without proper reference
